properties.remark: PEAK WIND GUST OF 64 MPH AT 415 PM ON WEDNESDAY APRIL 21.
REPORTED AT MONTROSE AIRPORT.
PEAK WIND GUST MEASURED AT RIL ASOS
ALONG HWY 92 AND CO RD 3050 NEAR HOTCHKISS...3 COTTONWOOD TREES DOWNED....ROOF OF A HAYSHED BLOWN OFF 200 TO 300 FT FROM SHED...AND POWER LINES DOWNED.
WIND SPEED ESTIMATED. LARGE TREE BRANCH DOWN NEAR HOTCHKISS.
RELAYED REPORTS OF COTTONWOOD TREES BLOWN OVER ON HIGHWAY 40 NEAR STEAMBOAT SPRINGS WITH THE JULY 14 STORM BETWEEN 8 AND 830 PM DUE TO GUSTY OUTFLOW AND STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.
DEBRIS OVER PARTS OF HWY 211 BETWEEN THE JCT OF 191 AND NEWSPAPER ROCK. ESTIMATED 6 INCHES OF WATER RUNNING OVER HWY. ALSO 0.25 HAIL ACCUMULATION UP TO 6 INCHES DEEP IN AREAS.
GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL REPORTED NEAR MM18 IN MESA VERDE NTL PARK AROUND 530 PM MDT.
STEVE DENNY REPORTED MULTIPLE MUDSLIDES ON HWY 145 AT MM 75 DUE TO HEAVY RAIN. HWY 145 CLOSED AT 500 PM MDT. RELAYED REPORT FROM WEBEOC

IN ADDITION TO 1.18 INCHES OF HEAVY RAIN IN 30 MINUTES... WIND GUSTS BROKE SOME TREE LIMBS AND KNOCKED OVER AN 18 INCH DIAMETER COTTONWOOD TREE IN RIVERSIDE PARK NEAR MONTROSE. NO MOBILE HOMES WERE IMPACTED.
HIGHWAY 145 AT NORWOOD HILL IS CLOSED
HIGHWAY 145 BETWEEN PLACERVILLE AND NORWOOD SAN MIGUEL CANYON W SEVERAL ROCK MUD SLIDES
MINOR LEAF DAMAGE.
WATER FLOWING INTO A NORMALLY DRY CULVERT JUST BEHIND THE ARCHES NP VISITORS CENTER THAT WAS RUNNING FULL AT 4 TO 5 FEET DEEP AND 4 TO 5 FEET WIDE FROM RECENT RAINS.
MEASURED AT THE RIFLE AIRPORT.
SMALL SHED BLOWN INTO A CAR AND LANDED ON TOP OF A FENCE.
MEASURED AT CANYONLANDS AIRPORT.
LOW WATER CROSSINGS AT WINTER CAMP AND SALT WASH CLOSED DUE TO WATER RUNNING ACROSS THE ROAD.

SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER FLOODED THE ISLAND IN THE SKY VISITOR CENTER BASEMENT. FLOODING WAS ALSO REPORTED OVER SHAFER CANYON OVERLOOK RD. HALF INCH HAIL ACCOMPANIED THE HEAVY RAIN. CURRENTLY RAINING LIGHTLY.
NORTHERN PORTION OF ROUTE 133 CLOSED DUE TO MUDSLIDE
A 30 TO 40 FOOT SECTION OF METAL ROOFING WAS LIFTED FROM THE STRUCTURE AND CARRIED ABOUT 200 MEET.
WINDS BLEW TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO 2 HOMES. DAMAGE REPORTS WERE 2ND HAND AND TIME IS ESTIMATED.
HWY 82 CLOSED DUE TO MUDSLIDE BETWEEN CARBONDALE AND GLENWOOD SPRINGS.
0.92 INCHES OF RAIN FELL WITHIN 15 MINUTES NEAR BLUFF. WASHES ARE FLOWING AND 6 INCHES OF STANDING WATER IN PARKING LOT WEST OF BLUFF. NO HAIL REPORTED.
20 TENTS BLOWN DOWN AT A CAMP SITE. FENCES BLOWN DOWN AND A METAL GATE BLOWN OFF BEHIND PARK SERVICE BUILDINGS.
HAIL FELL IN THE LEWIS AND ARRIOLA AREAS
0.75 TO 1 INCH DIAMETER HAIL COVERED THE GROUND.
POWER KNOCKED OUT IN PARTS OF TOWN.
HAIL CAUSED LEAF DAMAGE.
NUMEROUS TREES BLOWN DOWN LAST EVENING OVER UINTAH MOUNTAINS ALONG HIGHWAY 191 BETWEEN 8 AND 9 PM MDT.
